back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to the sons of Israel: When anyone sins unintentionally against any of the Lord's commandments which he is forbidden to do, and he does one of them—
If the anointed priest sins and causes the people to incur guilt as well, he shall present to the Lord a young bull without blemish, son of the herd, as a sin offering for the sin he has committed.
He shall bring the bull to the entrance of the tent of meeting before the face of the Lord, lay his hand on the head of the bull, and kill it before the face of the Lord.
And the anointed priest shall take some of the blood of the bull and bring it into the tent of meeting.
And the priest shall dip his finger into the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before the face of the Lord, in front of the veil of the sanctuary.

And the priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense which is before the face of the Lord in the tent of meeting, and all the rest of the blood of the bull he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
And he shall remove all the fat of the bull of the sin offering from it—the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails.
And both kidneys with the fat that is on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which he shall remove together with the kidneys—
Just as it is removed from the ox of the peace offering. And the priest shall burn these things on the altar of burnt offering.
But the hide of the bull and all its flesh, along with its head and its legs, and its entrails and its dung—
He shall take the entire bull outside the camp, to a clean place where the ashes are poured out, and burn it on wood with fire; at the place where the ashes are poured out, it shall be burned.

And if the whole congregation of Israel strays unintentionally and the matter is hidden from the eyes of the assembly, and they do one of any of the Lord's commandments which is forbidden to be done, and they incur guilt—
When they become aware of the sin they have committed, the assembly shall present a young bull, son of the herd, as a sin offering and bring it before the tent of meeting.
And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before the face of the Lord, and the bull shall be killed before the face of the Lord.
And the anointed priest shall bring some of the blood of the bull into the tent of meeting.
And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle seven times before the face of the Lord, in front of the veil.

And he shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar that is before the face of the Lord in the tent of meeting, and all the rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
And he shall remove all the fat from the bull and burn it as smoke on the altar.
He shall do to the bull just as he did to the bull of the sin offering—thus he shall do to it. And the priest shall make atonement for them, and they shall be forgiven.
And he shall take the bull outside the camp and burn it just as he burned the first bull. It is the sin offering of the assembly.
When a leader sins and does one of any of the commandments of the Lord his God which is forbidden to be done, unintentionally, and incurs guilt—
Or the sin he has committed is made known to him—he shall bring his offering, a male goat without blemish.
And he shall lay his hand on the head of the male goat and kill it at the place where the burnt offering is killed before the face of the Lord. It is a sin offering.

And the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering.
And he shall burn all its fat as smoke on the altar, like the fat of the peace offering. And the priest shall make atonement for him from his sin, and he shall be forgiven.
And if one person sins unintentionally from the common people, by doing one of the commandments of the Lord which is forbidden to be done, and incurs guilt—
Or the sin he has committed is made known to him—he shall bring his offering, a female goat without blemish, for the sin he has committed.
And he shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and kill the sin offering at the place of the burnt offering.
And the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and all the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.

And he shall remove all its fat, just as the fat is removed from the peace offering, and the priest shall burn it as smoke on the altar as a pleasing aroma to the Lord. And the priest shall make atonement for him, and he shall be forgiven.
And if he brings a lamb as his offering for a sin offering, he shall bring a female without blemish.
And he shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and kill it as a sin offering at the place where the burnt offering is killed.

And the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and all the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.

And he shall remove all its fat, just as the fat of the lamb is removed from the peace offering, and the priest shall burn these things as smoke on the altar along with the offerings by fire to the Lord. And the priest shall make atonement for him for the sin he has committed, and he shall be forgiven.
